note: Reviewed locally with the render-visual-reviewer rubric. The multi-agent tool policy in this environment permits spawning only when the user explicitly asks for sub-agents, so this is a local pure-visual review rather than an independent delegated review.

page_review.visible_evidence: Title, anonymous author line, abstract, and opening introduction fit without visible overlap or clipping.
page_review.main_risk: No figure on this page; visual strength depends on later evidence figures.

page_review.visible_evidence: Figure 1 v18 is readable at page scale: the four stages, VLM Checks title, Target: box label, and SR/SPL/NE tiles are visible; the caption clearly marks it as a schematic evidence-chain roadmap.
page_review.main_risk: The image is AI-generated schematic art, so claims must remain tied to real render/tables/log evidence.

page_review.visible_evidence: Related Work and Method opening are dense but stable; no obvious page-level overlap, clipped columns, or raw LaTeX artifacts.
page_review.main_risk: Text-heavy page with no visual anchor.

page_review.visible_evidence: Method and Evidence Gates sections fit with clean column flow and no visible float collision.
Table 1, VLM grounding results, and claim-boundary transition fit without visible overlap.
page_review.main_risk: Small table text is typical ACL dense-table size.

page_review.visible_evidence: Figure 2 uses real render evidence and remains visually aligned; Table 2 and Section 4.4 fit below it. The proxy and scene render pairs are comparable enough for orientation.
page_review.main_risk: The top proxy row is a cropped detail rather than a full-object view; the caption discloses that boundary.

page_review.visible_evidence: Tables 3-6 and the official-scene stability paragraph fit the page; no visible table/caption overlap.
page_review.main_risk: The page is dense, so the tables should remain treated as numeric claim artifacts rather than visual storytelling.

page_review.visible_evidence: Figure 3 material-effect panel is readable enough at page scale and shows Original MDL, ConvertAsset, and NVIDIA columns without the historical red material fallback; its caption bounds the claim to selected qualitative evidence.
page_review.main_risk: Selected visual evidence only; not a population-level NVIDIA failure-rate estimate.

page_review.visible_evidence: Figure 4 InternNav path panel is visible, caption stays attached, and Limitations/Ethical Considerations begin cleanly below; the widened left label column makes case IDs and SR O/N labels easier to scan while purple executed paths and green reference paths remain distinguishable.
page_review.main_risk: In-panel still details remain small because the figure preserves three selected cases across original/noMDL start/end columns; the caption and main text still carry the claim boundary.

page_review.visible_evidence: References flow across columns without visible clipping or missing bibliography chunks.
page_review.main_risk: Reference text is dense but normal for ACL.

page_review.visible_evidence: Final reference page is short but clean; no dangling figure, raw marker, or overflow visible.

minor_caveats: Figure 2 top proxy row is cropped by design.
Figure 4 path-panel labels are improved but still auxiliary at page scale; the selected stills and caption carry the visual explanation.
This was a local visual review, not an independent delegated review.
claim_boundary: Figure 1 is schematic roadmap art only.
Figure 2, Figure 3, and Figure 4 orient readers visually but do not replace the frozen tables, manifests, render logs, and claim-boundary gates.
Figure 3 supports selected covered-bin qualitative inspection only.
Figure 4 supports selected qualitative path inspection only; quantitative InternNav claims remain tied to the 99-episode paired run.
follow_up: Do not regenerate Figure 1 unless a new candidate has a specific visual advantage over v18 and passes text-accuracy review.
After the Figure 4 label polish, the next useful visual improvement would be optional appendix/supplement packaging rather than another main-paper figure replacement.
